meta.name: SOC-13 Sense of Coherence Scale
meta.description: The SOC-13 is a 13-item self-report measure that assesses an individual’s sense of coherence - the extent to which one perceives life as comprehensible, manageable, and meaningful. A strong sense of coherence is associated with better health outcomes and effective coping with stress.
meta.author: Antonovsky (1987)
meta.reference: Antonovsky A. Unraveling the mystery of health: How people manage stress and stay well. San Francisco: Jossey-Bass; 1987.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much you agree with each of the following statements. There are no right or wrong answers.

questions.text: Do you have the feeling that you don’t really care about what goes on around you?
Has it happened that you were surprised by the behavior of people whom you thought you knew well?
Has it happened that people whom you counted on disappointed you?
Until now your life has had: (1 = no clear goals or purpose, 7 = very clear goals and purpose)
Do you have the feeling that you’re being treated unfairly?
Do you have the feeling that you are in an unfamiliar situation and don’t know what to do?
Doing the things you do every day is: (1 = a source of pain and boredom, 7 = a source of pleasure and satisfaction)
Do you have very mixed-up feelings and ideas?
Does it happen that you have feelings inside you would rather not feel?
Many people sometimes feel like losers. How often have you felt this way?
When something happened, have you generally found that you saw things in the right proportion? (1 = over/underestimated, 7 = right proportion)
How often do you have the feeling that there’s little meaning in the things you do in your daily life?
How often do you have feelings that you’re not sure you can keep under control?

ranges.label: Very Low Coherence
ranges.description: Your sense of coherence is very low. You may find it difficult to see the world as comprehensible, manageable, and meaningful.
suggestions: Consider seeking professional support from a counselor or psychologist
Start with small, manageable goals to build a sense of control
Identify activities that give you a sense of meaning and purpose

ranges.label: Low Coherence
ranges.description: Your sense of coherence is below average. You may sometimes struggle to find meaning, predictability, or manageability in life events.
suggestions: Reflect on your personal values and what gives your life meaning
Develop problem-solving skills to enhance feelings of manageability
Seek social support when facing challenges
Engage in activities that provide structure and routine

ranges.label: Moderate Coherence
ranges.description: Your sense of coherence is at a moderate level. You generally find life comprehensible and manageable, with room for strengthening your sense of meaning.
suggestions: Continue building on your existing coping strategies
Seek new experiences that challenge and engage you
Strengthen social bonds and community connections
Practice gratitude and reflect on positive experiences

ranges.label: Strong Coherence
ranges.description: Your sense of coherence is strong. You tend to see life as comprehensible, manageable, and meaningful, which supports effective stress management.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your positive outlook and coping strategies
Share your resilience skills with others who may benefit
Stay engaged with meaningful activities and relationships
